steps.title: Keep verb and object together
steps.content: The blank supplies the verb for the subject, the Florida-based researchers. The words after the blank, "climate data from several sites," are the direct object, so no punctuation should separate the verb from that object.

steps.title: Reject punctuation breaks
steps.content: Choice A inserts a comma between verb and object. Choice C uses a semicolon before a noun phrase, not an independent clause. Choice D uses a colon where the object should attach directly to the verb. The answer is B.
